name: Vernon Brown
abstract: Vernon Brown (January 6, 1907 – May 18, 1979) was an American jazz trombonist that was the trombonist with Benny Goodman for 21 years, and helped participate in several major songs, such as Sing, Sing, Sing and One O'Clock Jump.

concerts.label: Benny Goodman and His Swing Orchestra
Jazz Concert by Benny Goodman and Louis Armstrong
Midnight Performance: Jazz Concert by Benny Goodman and Louis Armstrong
All-Star Jazz Concert and Recording Session

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Conniff
about_quotes.evidence: I think I played with three different Artie Shaw bands... beside such greats as Jack Jenney and Vernon Brown were in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Conniff interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Jerome
about_quotes.evidence: And so we went with him. Vernon Brown, myself, Nick Fatool, Les Robinson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Jerome interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: they were in the car with Lamar Wright, Vernon Brown and Chu, and of course all of us were at the bus... So Cab, that Christmas Cab would always buy the band a nice jacket... So the majority of the guys in the band had their jackets on that night.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: Hank D'Amico and Vernon Brown and Billy Butterfield and a bunch of them came over on the staff and they all were playing with Paul Lavalle so I was playing with them.

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Vernon Brown, but the interviewee (Monk Montgomery) does not respond or provide any personal anecdote or connection regarding him.

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Vernon Brown was there from the original band... And the trombones would be Red Ballard and Vernon Brown
